Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1. Search Airtable for the relocation records of all relevant employees using airtable_list_bases to discover the MoveOps operations base (appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001) to find the Relocations table (tblRelocations01), then airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to search for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics relocations assigned to Suki Patel. Find three records assigned to Suki and all showing Status "In Progress": recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF for Vectral Systems, Austin TX to San Francisco CA, move window April 16 to 22, ADA-accessible housing required for wheelchair user with roll-in shower and 36-inch doorways, split shipment with Heartland Movers handling household goods at approximately 6,000 lbs and Swift Relocations handling specialty items including a server rack at approximately 3,200 lbs; recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle for Vectral Systems, Boston MA to Seattle WA, move window April 12 to 14, hard April 12 pickup date with no flexibility, apartment pre-secured by Vectral at 1422 Minor Ave Apt 7B Seattle; and recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver for Mosaic Robotics, Tampa FL to Denver CO, move window April 14 to 19, hard delivery deadline of April 19 because the employee is lead presenter at the Automate 2026 trade show at the Denver Convention Center with booth setup beginning April 20. Also discover recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver for Vectral Systems, Portland OR to Denver CO, move window April 14 to 18, which is the third person in the Vectral April batch. Critically, Priya Suresh is assigned to Javier Morales, not Suki Patel, answering the prompt's question about whether the third Vectral employee is still on Suki's plate. Note that as of the current date (April 26), both Camille's and Deshawn's move windows have already passed while Terrence's window closed April 22, yet all three records still show In Progress with no recent updates.
OE2. Search Airtable for the stipend records using airtable_list_tables to find the Stipends table (tblStipends00001), then airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find Camille Oduya's stipend allocation record (recStipendAllocCamilleOduya). Discover that it shows the standard Vectral rate of $2,000 with Approval Status "Pending" and a note that the employee has not yet submitted any expenses. This is the key half of the stipend discrepancy. The other half comes from Camille's email in OE3.
OE3. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Terrence Nakamura" or "Nakamura" or similar, and separately "Camille Oduya" or "Oduya" or similar, and separately "Deshawn Hartley" or "Hartley" or "Denver" or similar) to find three critical unanswered emails in Suki's inbox. First, Terrence Nakamura's email about UPS batteries in his server rack: he has a CyberPower PR1500LCDRTXL2U rackmount UPS with two 12V/9Ah sealed lead-acid batteries (about 45 lbs total for the UPS unit) that are integral to the rack, he cannot remove them without risking data loss, and he wants to make sure the batteries are documented since lead-acid batteries sometimes have shipping restrictions. This email is unread (is_read: false) and Suki never responded. Second, Camille Oduya's email about her stipend: Rachel Whitfield told Camille during her offer negotiation that she would have a $3,500 equipment stipend available from day one when she arrives in Seattle, which is a special arrangement above the standard amount. Camille wants confirmation this is set up before she arrives April 14. This email is also unread and unanswered. Combining this with OE2, the agent discovers the stipend discrepancy: $3,500 promised by Rachel versus $2,000 configured in the system, and Suki cannot authorize the higher amount without account manager (Mina Hashimoto) sign-off. Third, Greg Pallone's email from Swift Relocations about the Deshawn Hartley truck breakdown: the 53-foot truck (unit SW-2247) assigned for the April 14 Tampa pickup blew a transmission coupling during the Tanaka Columbus-to-Seattle haul, with two options offered. Option A is a replacement unit from the Jacksonville depot with earliest Tampa pickup April 16 and estimated Denver delivery April 19 but tight. Option B is partner carrier Midwest Express with Tampa pickup April 15 and estimated Denver delivery April 20 to 21 but slower with no expedited routing. Suki never responded to this email, meaning no option was selected and the April 19 delivery deadline may have been missed.
OE4. Search the calendar using calendar_search_events (query: "Terrence" or "split shipment" or "coordination" or similar) or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (with a date range covering the relevant week) to find the vendor coordination call event (cal_terrence_split_shipment_call) titled "Terrence Nakamura Split Shipment Coordination, Swift + Heartland" with attendees Suki Patel, Greg Pallone at Swift, and Lisa Kwan at Heartland Movers. The event description covers finalizing the two-truck split shipment logistics, confirming Heartland pickup date for household goods, Swift pickup date for specialty items including the server rack, ADA housing delivery coordination with UrbanNest, and discussing hazmat documentation needed for the server rack UPS batteries flagged by Terrence on April 11. The agent should report whether this call has already happened or is upcoming based on the event timestamp.
OE5. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Suki" or "Deshawn" or "Terrence" or "Camille" or similar, filter_in_channel: C006 for the operations channel or broader) to find the message posted by Chloe Vance (user_id: moveops_chloe_vance) at timestamp 1777264800.000000 in the operations channel (C006) raising concerns about Suki's active moves. The message states Suki surfaced in the StormCloud reconstruction thread yesterday but has no status updates on her other active moves, and lists specific concerns: Deshawn Hartley had a hard April 19 delivery deadline and Greg from Swift flagged a truck breakdown on April 12 with Blessing mentioning she talked to Greg but no confirmation on rerouting; Terrence Nakamura has a complicated split shipment with Heartland and Swift plus ADA requirements with no updates since the move window opened April 16; and Camille Oduya had a hard April 12 pickup and her move window has passed with no status updates. Also find Blessing Okafor's message in C006 at timestamp 1776206100.000000 saying she spoke to Greg about the truck situation and he mentioned they are working on getting a replacement sorted for the Tampa pickup, and that she told him they would confirm by end of week.
OE6. Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Deshawn" or "Hartley" or "truck" or similar) to find the existing tracking ticket issue_deshawn_truck_reroute (number 501, title: "Deshawn Hartley, Swift truck breakdown, Tampa pickup rerouting needed"), assigned to Blessing Okafor with due date April 19, state in_progress, priority urgent. The description confirms the truck breakdown details and both options from Greg. Note that no equivalent Linear tickets exist for Terrence Nakamura's UPS battery compliance issue or Camille Oduya's stipend discrepancy, representing tracking gaps the agent should address.
OE7. Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria referencing "Vectral" or DocNumber "INV-2026-0410" or similar, and separately "Mosaic" or DocNumber "INV-2026-0411" or similar) to find two relevant invoices. First, inv_vectral_april_2026 (DocNumber: INV-2026-0410) for $15,900 covering the three-employee Vectral April batch including Priya Suresh standard relocation ($4,500) plus pet surcharge ($350), Terrence Nakamura standard relocation ($4,500) plus split-shipment surcharge ($900) plus ADA housing premium ($400), and Camille Oduya standard relocation ($4,500) plus nonstandard stipend platform fee ($750). Second, inv_mosaic_april_2026_3reloc (DocNumber: INV-2026-0411) for $9,010 covering three Mosaic relocations including apartment sourcing, flight booking, moving coordination, and stipend platform fee, plus non-standard surcharges pending client approval. The agent should verify these invoices match the actual state of the moves and flag any discrepancies, such as invoicing for completed services on moves that may not have been completed.
OE8. Look up contacts for relevant people using contacts_search_contacts (query: each name) to get email addresses needed before sending any outbound messages: chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (Vectral account manager), emeka.diallo@moveops.com (Mosaic account manager), terrence.nakamura@vectralsystems.com, camille.oduya@vectralsystems.com, and greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.
OE9. Update the three relocation records in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01) for recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF, recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle, and recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver. Add status notes reflecting the actual situation discovered during investigation: for Terrence, note the UPS battery compliance concern, the unreviewed hazmat documentation, and the missed or upcoming vendor coordination call; for Camille, note the stipend discrepancy ($3,500 promised versus $2,000 configured) requiring AM sign-off and the fact that the April 12 pickup window has passed; for Deshawn, note the truck breakdown, the two unselected options from Greg, and the fact that the April 19 trade show delivery deadline has passed with no confirmed rerouting.
OE10. Create Linear issues using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations) for the untracked blockers discovered during investigation. Create a ticket for the Terrence Nakamura UPS battery compliance and hazmat documentation issue, since no Linear ticket exists for this despite it being a regulatory concern flagged by the employee. Create a ticket for the Camille Oduya stipend discrepancy requiring Mina Hashimoto's sign-off before configuration, since this is an unresolved exception with no tracking. Deshawn's truck rerouting already has a ticket (issue_deshawn_truck_reroute, number 501) but the agent should verify its status is still accurate and update it if needed.
OE11. Post a status update to the operations channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C006) summarizing the findings across all three moves: Terrence Nakamura's UPS battery compliance issue and the vendor coordination call status, Camille Oduya's stipend discrepancy and the passed move window, Deshawn Hartley's truck breakdown with no rerouting decision made and the passed trade show deadline, and that the third Vectral employee (Priya Suresh) is confirmed as Javier's assignment. Include the QuickBooks invoicing status and any other relevant findings. This directly addresses the prompt's request to post in the operations channel so Chloe has visibility.
OE12. Send email notifications using send_email (sender: suki.patel@moveops.com) to Chloe Vance and the relevant account managers about the urgent issues discovered. Email Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) about the Camille Oduya stipend discrepancy ($3,500 versus $2,000) requesting her sign-off as the Vectral account manager, and flagging the overall status of both Vectral relocations. Email Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) about the Deshawn Hartley situation since he is the Mosaic account manager and needs to know the trade show delivery deadline may have been missed due to the unresolved truck rerouting. If the issues are serious enough, also email Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com) directly with a summary of the most critical gaps, particularly the Deshawn delivery deadline and the Terrence compliance concern.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Email Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) about the Camille Oduya stipend discrepancy ($3,500 versus $2,000) requesting her sign-off as the Vectral account manager, and flagging the overall status of both Vectral relocations. Email Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com) about the Deshawn Hartley situation since he is the Mosaic account manager and needs to know the trade show delivery deadline may have been missed due to the unresolved truck rerouting. If the issues are serious enough, also email Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com) directly with a summary of the most critical gaps, particularly the Deshawn delivery deadline and the Terrence compliance concern.
